title: Project Report: CLI Task Manager with SQLite — Python Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Command-line todo app with SQLite persistence, argparse, and pytest coverage.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**CLI Task Manager with SQLite** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for Python Developer. Duration: 1–2 weeks. Command-line todo app with SQLite persistence, argparse, and pytest coverage. Tools: Python 3.12, sqlite3, pytest.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: CRUD tasks from terminal
Objective: SQLite schema with migrations
Objective: pytest unit tests
Objective: Package with pyproject.toml
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Python Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Python Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Python Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Schema
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Schema** *(Level: Beginner)*

Design tasks table. Complete these tasks: CREATE TABLE; Seed data. Deliverable for this phase: schema.sql.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] CREATE TABLE
- [ ] Seed data
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Schema is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it

### Real use example
Team demo shows the schema.sql to the teacher.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: GitHub repo; Coverage report; README with usage examples. Skills demonstrated: CLI, SQLite, pytest. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: GitHub repo
Deliverable: Coverage report
Deliverable: README with usage examples
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits GitHub repo and passes the rubric.
